message:
🌱 jaeger: Support for Elasticsearch ILM policies (#1151)

* 🌱 Add jaeger: Support for Elasticsearch ILM policies mission

Signed-off-by: github-actions[bot] <41898282+github-actions[bot]@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: correct jaeger namespace to observability

Signed-off-by: Andrew Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>

---------

files.patch:
@@ -0,0 +1,73 @@
+{
+  "version": "kc-mission-v1",
+  "name": "jaeger-2048-support-for-elasticsearch-ilm-policies",
+  "missionClass": "solution",
+  "author": "KubeStellar Bot",
+  "authorGithub": "kubestellar",
+  "mission": {
+    "title": "jaeger: Support for Elasticsearch ILM policies",
+    "description": "Support for Elasticsearch ILM policies. Requested by 7+ users.",
+    "type": "feature",
+    "status": "completed",
+    "steps": [
+      {
+        "title": "Check current jaeger deployment",
+        "description": "Verify your jaeger version and configuration:\n```bash\nkubectl get pods -n observability -l app.kubernetes.io/name=jaeger\nhelm list -n observability 2>/dev/null || echo \"Not installed via Helm\"\n```\nThis feature requires a working jaeger installation."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Review jaeger configuration",
+        "description": "Inspect the relevant jaeger configuration:\n```bash\nkubectl get all -n observability -l app.kubernetes.io/name=jaeger\nkubectl get configmap -n observability -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=jaeger\n```\n## Requirement - what kind of business use case are you trying to solve?\n\nI want to define index lifecycle policy to manage jaeger indicies in Elasticsearch. Purpose for me is to set policy that will delete outdated indices."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Apply the fix for Support for Elasticsearch ILM policies",
+        "description": "## Which problem is this PR solving?\n- Resolves #2048\n## Short description of the changes\n- Adds support for ILM policies by creating overriding index templates - which assign the ILM policy and rollover alias and read-alias to the index upon creation. Change is made esRollover script to enable ILM using an environment variable USE_ILM to be backwards compatible.\n\nSee the source issue for community-verified solutions."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Verify the feature works",
+        "description": "Test that the new capability is working as expected:\n```bash\nkubectl get pods -n observability -l app.kubernetes.io/name=jaeger\nkubectl get events -n observability --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10\n```\nConfirm the feature described in \"Support for Elasticsearch ILM policies\" is functioning correctly."
+      }
+    ],
+    "resolution": {
+      "summary": "## Which problem is this PR solving?\n- Resolves #2048\n## Short description of the changes\n- Adds support for ILM policies by creating overriding index templates - which assign the ILM policy and rollover alias and read-alias to the index upon creation. Change is made esRollover script to enable ILM using an environment variable USE_ILM to be backwards compatible.",
+      "codeSnippets": []
+    }
+  },
